commit.message:
feat: production hardening — reliability, performance, observability, infra

Backend:
- Add structured JSON logging with request ID tracing (logger.ts)
- Add graceful shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT) with connection draining
- Add process.on(unhandledRejection/uncaughtException) crash guards
- Health check now verifies DB + Redis connectivity, returns 503 if down
- Background jobs wrapped with error handling (.catch on all imports)
- Scheduled expired refresh token cleanup (every 6 hours)
- Analytics: replace all findMany→in-memory with DB aggregation (prevents OOM)
  - Daily revenue/orders via GROUP BY DATE
  - PageView analytics via COUNT(DISTINCT sessionId), GROUP BY country/path
  - Order funnel via groupBy paymentStatus
  - Hourly orders via EXTRACT(HOUR)
  - Previous period via aggregate() instead of findMany()
- Prisma client: add query logging for dev, error/warn for production
- Abandoned cart queries: add orderBy to prevent reprocessing same records

Database:
- Add missing indexes: Order(paymentStatus, createdAt, composite),
  OrderItem(productId, orderId), Payment(status, orderId),
  Cart(createdAt, updatedAt), User(createdAt, role)

Frontend:
- ServiceWorker: register SW properly instead of unregistering all (fixes PWA)
- API client: 30s AbortController timeout on all requests
- API client: exponential backoff retry (3 attempts) for 5xx/network errors
- API client: respect caller AbortSignal for request cancellation

Infrastructure:
- Docker: Redis AOF persistence + 256MB maxmemory + LRU eviction + volume
- Docker: PostgreSQL tuning (shared_buffers, work_mem, max_connections, etc.)
- Docker: CPU/memory resource limits on all services
- Docker: DB connection pool params (connection_limit=20, pool_timeout=30s)
- Docker: internal network isolation (only nginx exposed)
- Nginx: gzip compression for text/css/js/json/svg/woff2
- Nginx: static asset caching (30d, immutable)
- Nginx: proxy buffering, rate limiting zones, SSL session cache/stapling
- Nginx: separate API route block with rate limiting
- Nginx: additional security headers (Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
- Dockerfile: strict TypeScript build (no error suppression)
- Dockerfile: production-only deps (npm ci --omit=dev) — smaller image
- Dockerfile: prisma migrate deploy instead of db push --accept-data-loss
- Dockerfile: run as non-root user (appuser:nodejs)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: passive queue model — let Yandex control queue advancement

MA is a passive player that doesn't know the queue. Previously,
_advance_queue() manipulated current_playable_index and sent
update_full_state with is_currently_active=False, which:
1. Took queue control away from the YM app
2. Reset active device status on each track change
3. Caused 'next track' to become unavailable after auto-advance

Now replaced with _signal_track_completion() which only:
- Sends update_playing_status(progress=duration, paused=False)
- Sends sync_state_from_eov() to request queue replenishment
- Waits for Yandex to push the next track via Ynison

Also adds update_player_state() to YnisonClient for queue changes
that preserve active device status (_on_previous still needs it).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial implementation of starlette-gcp-logging

Written by GitHub Copilot.

Provides structured JSON logging for Starlette services on Google Cloud Run:

- GCPFormatter: logging.Formatter that emits single-line JSON with GCP
  severity mapping, source location, and trace/span correlation via
  logging.googleapis.com/* keys
- GCPRequestLoggingMiddleware: Starlette middleware that logs one
  httpRequest entry per request/response and propagates trace context
  to all loggers via contextvars (no manual plumbing required)
- Trace context extracted from traceparent (W3C, preferred) or
  X-Cloud-Trace-Context (GCP fallback)
- Project ID auto-detected from GCP instance metadata server via
  functools.cache, with graceful fallback outside GCP
- GCPFormatter and GCPRequestLoggingMiddleware exported at the top-level
  package; submodules (formatter, middleware, _metadata) also accessible
- GitHub Actions workflows for CI (quality) and PyPI release
- pre-commit config with ruff lint/format hooks
- unittest-based test suite

commit.message:
Agent infrastructure: container tooling, review quality, and observability (#58)

## Summary

Overhaul of the agent infrastructure across three areas: container
setup, review pipeline quality, and debugging observability.

### Container & workflows
- Pre-install mise + all dev tools (ruff, ty, pytest, shellcheck,
actionlint) in agent Docker image
- Widen build context to repo root with `.dockerignore`
- Make both workflows (code-review, implement) fire-and-forget — agent
handles async via GitHub API
- CLI timeout raised to 1800s (30 min safety net) — previous 600s killed
productive implement runs

### Review pipeline
- Inject PR title, description, base branch, and linked issue bodies
into review prompt
- Pydantic `ReviewOutput`/`ReviewComment` models replace 45 lines of
manual dict validation
- `--share` flag captures full CLI session transcript for post-mortem
debugging

### Review skill (bot-review)
- Structured comment format: `{severity} — Pattern name` +
Problem/Impact/Fix
- Strategic review approach: big picture first, blast radius assessment
- Concise body summary (design direction, not file listing)
- Rich JSON examples modeling all severity levels

### Tests
- 68 tests total (up from 49)
- New: linked issue parsing (11), async fetch (4), start_line support
(1), CLI --share (3)

### Other
- Stats line always visible in review comments (removed `<details>`
wrapper)
- Write-issue skill: added "Triggering implementation" section

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

commit.message:
Initial commit: Dungeon Robber HTML remake

OD&D dungeon crawler based on Paul Hughes' Flash game (Blog of Holding).
Single-file HTML, no dependencies.

Features:
- 5 unlockable classes (Rogue, Fighter, Thief, Cleric, Magic-User)
- 15 monster types with special abilities (poison, paralysis, level drain, undead turning)
- Procedural dungeon graph (BSP-style lazy generation)
- Full OD&D d20 combat with saving throws
- Town progression system (4 levels, 7 buildings)
- Hall of Heroes (localStorage)
- Mobile-friendly parchment UI
